rulings.Case Title: PERFORMING ARTS LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MICHAEL MURRAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Quash For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, April 22, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTs LEO CASSIDY, JAMES NUNEMACHER, THE CHRIS JAMES CORPORATION DBA VAN GUARD's Motion To Quash For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: UNIFUND CCR, LLC VS. RODOLFO R. CISNEROS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 22, 2014, line 6. PLAINTIFF UNIFUND CCR, LLC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. The motion is denied without prejudice. The moving party failed to provide a courtesy copy of the Requests for Admissions, the Motion for Order to Deem Matters Admitted, and the order deeming matters admitted. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. These documents are not scanned on the Court's register of actions. In fact, the register of actions does not show that the Judge Pro Tem ever executed a written order deeming the matters admitted. The Court cannot grant judgment on the pleadings based on the present record before it. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK SMITH VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 22, 2014, line 8. DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion to strike the demand for punitive damages is granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff seeks punitive damages at paragraph 14(a)(2) of the Complaint. As a matter of law, plaintiff cannot recover punitive damages against The Regents of the University of California. See Gov't Code sections 811.2 and 818. No opposition was filed. rulings.Case Title: IXC HOLDINGS, INC., DBA TELEKENEX VS. LEGALMATCH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 22, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT LEGALMATCH'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the First Amended Complaint is overruled. On its face, the First Amended Complaint does not show that the breach of contract cause of action is time-barred. See E-Fab, Inc. v. Accountants, Inc. Svcs. (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 1308, 1315-16. Defendant raises factual issues that cannot be considered on demurrer. rulings.Case Title: CHI WONG et al VS. BILL WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 22, 2014, line 14. DEFENDANTS BILL WONG, CHOR MAN WONG, WING TAT WONG, CHUNG WAH WONG MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is granted as to the following phrase in paragraph 2 of the Second Amended Complaint: "as well as in their own individual capacities as members and officers of the association." Plaintiffs Chi Wong and Jackson Wong lack standing to sue, as no harm has been alleged to either plaintiff as an individual. (CCP §367.) Plaintiff has already been given leave to amend to allege facts showing harm to individuals by Order fled December 3, 2013, and did not do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GEORGE A. MCKRAY et al VS. U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Have Pltf George A Mcray Declared A Vexatious Litigant
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 22, 2014, line 12. DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP, DORSEY & WHITNEY LLP, NICHOLAS VLIETSTRA, JUAN BASOMBRIO, KENT SCHMIDT, U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Have Plaintiff George A Mcray Declared A Vexatious Litigant. Denied without prejudice. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Defendants seek to declare Plaintiff McKray a vexatious litigant pursuant to CCP sec. 391(b)(2). Although he has filed multiple lawsuits on behalf of Plaintiffs Anthony Lamperti and Elizabeth Lamperti, this is the second lawsuit where he is a pro per plaintiff. rulings.Case Title: GEORGE A. MCKRAY et al VS. U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 22, 2014, line 12. DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP, DORSEY & WHITNEY LLP, NICHOLAS VLIETSTRA, JUAN BASOMBRIO, KENT SCHMIDT, U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted. Defendants' unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiffs' Request for Judicial Notice of Exhibit 3 in Support of Complaint is granted. The allegations in the Complaint arise from Defendants engaging in litigation by filing anti-SLAPP motions and motions for statutory attorneys' fees. (Complaint, p. 1.) Engaging in litigation is a protected activity. (CCP sec. 425.16(e)(1).) Plaintiffs have not met their burden of showing a possibility of prevailing. The Complaint seeks to have the trial court vacate final decisions of the Court of Appeal. Plaintiffs cite no authority that would allow for such relief. Defendants are awarded reasonable attorneys' fees in the amount of $13,374.00 pursuant to CCP sec. 425.16(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH MASTERS VS. WHITE OAK GLOBAL ADVISOR, LL,
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Seal The Request For Judicial Notice And The Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Filed In Support Of Demurrers
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 22, 2014, line 13. DEFENDANTS BARBARA MCKEE, ANDRE HAKKAK, WHITE OAK GLOBAL ADVISORS, LLC'S Motion To Seal The Request For Judicial Notice And The Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Filed In Support Of Demurrers. Grant. The Request for Judicial Notice and the Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of Defendants Demurrers to Complaint of Kenneth Masters and the Request for Judicial Notice made in connection with Defendants' Demurrers to Complaint of Kenneth Masters, and lodged conditionally under seal on March 24, 2014, shall be filed under seal. The Court finds there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record (a confidential settlement agreement in another matter); the overriding interest supports sealing the records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored in that only the settlement agreement itself (Exhibit A) and references to it in the Memorandum of Points and Authorities will be sealed; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. No opposition was filed to the motion. The motion complies with Rule of Court 2.550 et seq. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order that complies with CRC 2.550 and 2.551 and states the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
